title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: vignette walks complete nhanesR workflow using concrete example: association serum total cholesterol -cause mortality across ten NHANES cycles (1999–2018), adjusting HDL cholesterol, prior myocardial infarction, cholesterol-lowering medication use. None code chunks run automatically — copy paste one R console run interactively. Downloaded files cached locally, re-running step fast first time. workflow nine steps: Browse available cycles files Discover variables nhanes_search_variables() nhanes_variable_map() Download laboratory data nhanes_download_analyte() Download questionnaire data (MI history, cholesterol medication) Harmonize variable names units across cycles nhanes_harmonize() Recode questionnaire variables Merge components demographics Link mortality follow-prepare survival dataset Fit survey-weighted Cox proportional hazards model
code: library(nhanesR) library(survival) library(survey)

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: Three options control nhanesR behaviour. package sets defaults load time, option defined .Rprofile loading takes precedence. Default cache locations platform: make changes permanent, add lines like ~/.Rprofile: check change settings interactively session:
code: options(   nhanesR.cache_dir = "/data/nhanes_cache",  # e.g. a shared server path   nhanesR.verbose   = FALSE,   nhanesR.timeout   = 300L ) # View current cache location nhanes_cache_dir()  # Change for this session only nhanes_cache_dir("~/my_nhanes_cache")  # Suppress download messages for this session options(nhanesR.verbose = FALSE)

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: NHANES (National Health Nutrition Examination Survey) conducted two-year cycles (e.g. 1999–2000, 2001–2002, …, 2017–2018). Within cycle, data organized five components: participant unique identifier, SEQN, links files within cycle. SEQNs reused across cycles, always include "cycle" merge keys pooling multiple cycles.

what: 1. What cycles and files are available?
title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: see files available specific cycle component, use nhanes_manifest():
code: # All continuous NHANES cycles known to nhanesR nhanes_cycles()  # Just the cycle labels for the first ten continuous cycles (1999-2018) cycles <- nhanes_cycles()[1:10, "cycle"] cycles nhanes_manifest("2015-2016", "Laboratory") nhanes_manifest("2013-2014", "Questionnaire")

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: NHANES analytes often stored different variable names different cycles. nhanes_search_variables() searches CDC variable catalog keyword. nhanes_variable_map() returns one-row-per-cycle lookup table showing exact file name variable name use. nhanes_variable_map() output directly informs keep_vars argument used download harmonization .
code: # Find total cholesterol across all cycles (summarized by default) nhanes_search_variables("total cholesterol", component = "Laboratory")  # Raw one-row-per-cycle output nhanes_search_variables("total cholesterol", component = "Laboratory",                          summarize = FALSE) # Per-cycle lookup: which file and variable name holds total cholesterol? nhanes_variable_map("total cholesterol")  # HDL changed variable name three times across cycles nhanes_variable_map("HDL")  # Questionnaire: history of MI (keep_vars filters out false positives) nhanes_variable_map("heart attack", component = "Questionnaire",                      keep_vars = c("MCQ160E", "MCQ160e"))

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: nhanes_download_analyte() uses variable catalog look correct CDC file name cycle, downloads . resolves cross-cycle file renames automatically — example, total cholesterol LAB13 (1999–2000), L13_B (2001–2002), L13_C (2003–2004), TCHOL_D onward. Files downloaded SAS transport (XPT) format, parsed, cached locally. Subsequent calls load cache.
code: cycles <- nhanes_cycles()[1:10, "cycle"]  # 1999-2018  # Demographics — file name has always been DEMO; nhanes_download() works fine demo_list <- nhanes_download("DEMO", cycles)  # Total cholesterol — file renamed across early cycles; use download_analyte() tchol_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("total cholesterol", cycles)  # HDL cholesterol hdl_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("HDL", cycles)

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: nhanes_download_analyte() function works component. Use keep_vars search term otherwise match false positives.
code: # History of myocardial infarction (MCQ file) # MCQ160E (1999-2010) and MCQ160e (2011-2018) are the same question; # keep_vars filters out RXQ510 which also mentions "heart attack" mi_list <- nhanes_download_analyte(   "heart attack", cycles,   component = "Questionnaire",   keep_vars = c("MCQ160E", "MCQ160e") )  # Cholesterol-lowering medication (BPQ file) # "Ever told to take prescribed medicine to lower blood cholesterol?" chol_med_list <- nhanes_download_analyte(   "cholesterol", cycles,   component = "Questionnaire",   keep_vars = c("BPQ090D", "BPQ101D") )

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: nhanes_harmonize() renames per-cycle variables single common name optionally stacks cycles one data frame. Unit-based harmonization (laboratory data): specify unit name; function finds right column cycle matching label attribute, variable codes needed. prefer_mgdl = TRUE (default) drops mmol/L duplicates automatically. trim = TRUE (default) returns SEQN, cycle, target column — ready merging. Mapping-based harmonization (questionnaire data): use mapping unit match. trim = TRUE default applies.
code: # Total cholesterol — LBXTC throughout, but label_pattern narrows the match # in 1999-2004 when TC and HDL were bundled in the same file TC <- nhanes_harmonize(   tchol_list,   unit          = "mg/dL",   name          = "TC_mgdl",   label_pattern = "total cholesterol" )  # HDL — three different variable names across cycles; unit approach handles all HDL <- nhanes_harmonize(   hdl_list,   unit          = "mg/dL",   name          = "HDL_mgdl",   label_pattern = "HDL" )  str(TC)   # SEQN (chr), cycle (chr), TC_mgdl (num) str(HDL)  # SEQN (chr), cycle (chr), HDL_mgdl (num) MI <- nhanes_harmonize(   mi_list,   mapping = c(MCQ160E = "MI_history", MCQ160e = "MI_history") )  chol_med <- nhanes_harmonize(   chol_med_list,   mapping = c(BPQ090D = "chol_med", BPQ101D = "chol_med") )  # Each result is a trim 3-column data frame ready for merging str(MI)        # SEQN, cycle, MI_history str(chol_med)  # SEQN, cycle, chol_med

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: NHANES questionnaire responses use numeric coding convention: analysis, recode 0/1 treat 7 9 NA:
code: nhanes_recode_yn <- function(x) {   out        <- rep(NA_integer_, length(x))   out[x == 1] <- 1L   out[x == 2] <- 0L   out }  MI$MI_history      <- nhanes_recode_yn(MI$MI_history) chol_med$chol_med  <- nhanes_recode_yn(chol_med$chol_med)  # Verify: should see 0, 1, and NA only table(MI$MI_history,      useNA = "always") table(chol_med$chol_med,  useNA = "always")

what: 7. Stack demographics and merge all components
title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: Stack per-cycle demographics list, merge components SEQN cycle. Use .x = TRUE (left join) demographics outward participants without lab values retained NA.
code: demo <- nhanes_stack(demo_list)  # Inner join lab data (keeps only participants who attended the exam) analytic <- Reduce(   function(a, b) merge(a, b, by = c("SEQN", "cycle")),   list(demo, TC, HDL) )  # Left join questionnaire data (all interviewed participants have these) analytic <- merge(analytic, MI,       by = c("SEQN", "cycle"), all.x = TRUE) analytic <- merge(analytic, chol_med, by = c("SEQN", "cycle"), all.x = TRUE)  nrow(analytic) names(analytic)  # Check key variables arrived c("TC_mgdl", "HDL_mgdl", "MI_history", "chol_med",   "RIDAGEYR", "RIAGENDR", "WTMEC2YR", "SDMVPSU", "SDMVSTRA") %in%   names(analytic)

what: 8. Link mortality and prepare the survival dataset
title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: nhanes_mortality_link() downloads NCHS Public-Use Linked Mortality Files left-joins SEQN. Follow-runs December 31, 2019. nhanes_survival_prep() removes ineligible participants, creates time event columns, warns asymmetric follow-across cycles. Use origin = "exam" laboratory measurements exposure — collected exam visit. cause-specific mortality:
code: analytic_mort <- nhanes_mortality_link(analytic)  # Key variables added: #   ELIGSTAT     1=eligible, 2=under 18, 3=insufficient data for linkage #   MORTSTAT     0=assumed alive 31-Dec-2019, 1=assumed deceased #   UCOD_LEADING Underlying cause of death (11-category ICD-10 recode) #   PERMTH_EXM   Months from examination date to death or Dec 31 2019 #   PERMTH_INT   Same, from interview date  table(analytic_mort$MORTSTAT, useNA = "always") surv_data <- nhanes_survival_prep(   analytic_mort,   origin     = "exam",   time_unit  = "years",   weight_var = "WTMEC2YR" )  # Follow-up by cycle — note shrinking maximum as cycles approach 2019 nhanes_followup_summary(surv_data) nhanes_ucod_labels()   # see available cause-of-death codes  surv_cvd <- nhanes_survival_prep(   analytic_mort,   origin     = "exam",   time_unit  = "years",   cause      = "001",   # Diseases of heart   weight_var = "WTMEC2YR" )  table(event = surv_cvd$event, cvd_death = surv_cvd$event_cause)

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: NHANES uses complex multi-stage probability sample. Standard errors must account sampling design anti-conservative. Weight adjustment pooled cycles: divide two-year exam weight WTMEC2YR number cycles pooled (, 10). nest = TRUE correct specification NHANES — PSU labels may repeat across strata. Fit Cox model -cause mortality adjusting age, sex, HDL, prior MI, cholesterol-lowering medication: Interpreting output: TC_sd: hazard ratio per one-SD higher total cholesterol, adjusted covariates. direction often reverses adjusting HDL statin use — important confounding structure lipid epidemiology. HDL_sd: higher HDL typically protective (HR < 1). RIAGENDR: coded 1 = male, 2 = female; HR compares females males. MI_history: coded 1 = prior MI, 0 = none; HR estimates excess mortality risk history heart attack. Confounding indication: participants prescribed statin typically higher pre-treatment cholesterol greater cardiovascular risk, statin users sicker average measured TC suggests. Exposure mismeasurement: statins lower TC approximately 30–40 mg/dL, measured TC treated individuals systematically underestimates underlying lipid burden. Including chol_med covariate adjusts group difference recover pre-treatment value. Common analytic responses include restricting analysis untreated participants, imputing pre-treatment TC adding back estimated treatment effect, stratifying medication status reporting separate associations.
code: surv_data$wt_pooled <- surv_data$survey_weight / 10  # Scale continuous predictors to per-SD units for interpretable hazard ratios surv_data$TC_sd  <- scale(surv_data$TC_mgdl)[,  1] surv_data$HDL_sd <- scale(surv_data$HDL_mgdl)[, 1]  design <- svydesign(   id      = ~SDMVPSU,   strata  = ~SDMVSTRA,   weights = ~wt_pooled,   nest    = TRUE,   data    = surv_data ) fit <- svycoxph(   Surv(time, event) ~ TC_sd + HDL_sd + RIDAGEYR + RIAGENDR +                       MI_history + chol_med,   design = design )  summary(fit) round(exp(cbind(HR = coef(fit), confint(fit))), 3)

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: Many NHANES analytes changed variable names file names across cycles. Use nhanes_search_variables() discover exists, nhanes_variable_map() get per-cycle file names, nhanes_download_analyte() + nhanes_harmonize() download rename consistently:
code: # General pattern for any analyte analyte_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("search term", cycles,                                          component = "Laboratory") analyte      <- nhanes_harmonize(analyte_list,                                   unit  = "mg/dL",                                   name  = "my_variable",                                   label_pattern = "search term")  # For questionnaire variables (no unit to match), use mapping instead quest_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("keyword", cycles,                component = "Questionnaire",                keep_vars = c("VAR_OLD", "VAR_NEW")) quest <- nhanes_harmonize(quest_list,            mapping = c(VAR_OLD = "my_flag", VAR_NEW = "my_flag"))

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: nhanesR automatically converts SEQN character download. Never use arithmetic. Always include "cycle" second join key merging pooled multi-cycle data — SEQNs unique within cycle.

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: Yes/questionnaire items use: 1 = Yes, 2 = , 7 = Refused, 9 = know. Always recode 0/1 analysis set 7 9 NA.

title: NHANES Mortality Linkage: A Complete Workflow
text: Asymmetric follow-: public-use LMF files censor December 31, 2019 regardless cycle. Participants 2017–2018 ~2 years follow-; 1999–2000 ~20 years. Data perturbation: PERMTH_EXM, PERMTH_INT, UCOD_LEADING contain synthetic values select records reduce re-identification risk. MORTSTAT ELIGSTAT perturbed. Restricted-use files: 2022-linked files extend follow-December 31, 2022. require approved project RDC access. reading: CDC mortality linkage: https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data-linkage/mortality-public.htm NHANES analytic guidelines: https://wwwn.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/analyticguidelines.aspx survey package: https://r-survey.r-forge.r-project.org/survey/

title: Getting Started with nhanesR
text: nhanesR provides structured workflow downloading, caching, harmonizing, analyzing data National Health Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES), including linkage NCHS Public-Use Linked Mortality Files (LMF). package handles main friction points working NHANES: File names change across cycles (e.g. total cholesterol: LAB13 → L13_B → L13_C → TCHOL_D onward). Variable names change across cycles (e.g. HDL: LBDHDL → LBXHDD → LBDHDD). SI-unit duplicates appear file alongside conventional-unit columns. Mortality linkage requires fixed-width file parsing SEQN joining across cycles.

title: Download, Parse, and Analyze NHANES Data with Mortality Linkage
code: library(nhanesR)  # 1. Find available cycles cycles <- nhanes_cycles()[["cycle"]]   # character vector of all cycle labels  # 2. Browse files available in a cycle nhanes_manifest("2015-2016", "Laboratory")  # 3. Search the variable catalog by keyword nhanes_search_variables("total cholesterol", component = "Laboratory") nhanes_variable_map("total cholesterol")   # per-cycle file/variable lookup  # 4. Download — use nhanes_download_analyte() when file names changed across cycles demo_list  <- nhanes_download("DEMO", cycles[1:10])          # stable name tchol_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("total cholesterol",   # resolves renames                                        cycles[1:10])  # 5. Harmonize variable names and stack into one data frame TC <- nhanes_harmonize(tchol_list,                         unit          = "mg/dL",                         name          = "TC_mgdl",                         label_pattern = "total cholesterol")  # 6. Merge components by SEQN demo   <- nhanes_stack(demo_list) analytic <- nhanes_merge(demo, TC, by = c("SEQN", "cycle"))  # 7. Link mortality follow-up (through December 31, 2019) analytic_mort <- nhanes_mortality_link(analytic)  # 8. Prepare survival dataset surv_data <- nhanes_survival_prep(analytic_mort,                                    origin     = "exam",                                    time_unit  = "years",                                    weight_var = "WTMEC2YR")

what: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
text: Downloads one NHANES component files SAS transport (XPT) format CDC website, parses R data frames, attaches variable labels, caches results locally RDS files.

title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
code: nhanes_download(file_code, cycles, refresh = FALSE, add_cycle_col = TRUE)

title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
text: file_code Character. NHANES file code(s), without suffix extension (e.g. "DEMO", "BPX", "TRIGLY"). Case-insensitive. Can vector download multiple files. cycles Character. One cycle labels (e.g. "2015-2016"). See nhanes_cycles(). multiple cycles given, file code downloaded . refresh Logical. Re-download re-parse even cached? Default FALSE. add_cycle_col Logical. Add cycle column returned data frame? Default TRUE. Required nhanes_mortality_link().

title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
text: single file_code single cycle requested, data frame. multiple file_codes cycles requested, named list data frames names form "{file_code}_{cycle}".

title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
text: File codes base names CDC assigns data file, without cycle-letter suffix .xpt extension. example, Demographics file always "DEMO", blood pressure examination file "BPX". Use nhanes_manifest() browse files available given cycle component. file_name column manifest shows full CDC name including cycle suffix (e.g. "TCHOL_I"); strip trailing underscore-letter get base code nhanes_download():   Note analyte file names changed across cycles (e.g. total cholesterol: LAB13 → L13_B → TCHOL_D onward). cases, use nhanes_download_analyte() instead, looks correct CDC filename cycle automatically via variable catalog.
code: m <- nhanes_manifest("2015-2016", "Laboratory") m[, c("file_name", "description")]  # Base codes ready for nhanes_download(): sub("_[A-Z]$", "", m$file_name)

title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
text: File codes validated download attempt. unknown code supplied, CDC returns HTTP 200 HTML error page rather 404. nhanesR detects via Content-Type header aborts message directing nhanes_manifest() confirm correct name.

title: Download and cache NHANES XPT data files — nhanes_download
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Browse available Laboratory files for a cycle, then download by base code m <- nhanes_manifest("2015-2016", "Laboratory") m[, c("file_name", "description")]   # see what's available bpx <- nhanes_download("BPX", "2015-2016")  # Single file, single cycle demo <- nhanes_download("DEMO", "2015-2016")  # Multiple cycles (returns list) demos <- nhanes_download("DEMO", c("2013-2014", "2015-2016", "2017-2018"))  # Multiple files, single cycle files <- nhanes_download(c("DEMO", "BPX", "TRIGLY"), "2015-2016") } # }

what: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
title: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
text: NHANES analytes sometimes stored different variable names different cycles (e.g. HDL cholesterol: LBDHDL 1999-2002, LBXHDD 2003-2004, LBDHDD 2005 onward). nhanes_harmonize() offers two ways resolve :

title: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
code: nhanes_harmonize(   data_list,   mapping = NULL,   unit = NULL,   name = NULL,   label_pattern = NULL,   prefer_mgdl = TRUE,   trim = TRUE,   stack = TRUE )

title: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
text: data_list named list per-cycle data frames, returned nhanes_download() nhanes_download_analyte(). mapping named character vector names old (per-cycle) variable names values single common name use across cycles. Multiple old names may map new name. Example: c(LBDHDL = "HDL_mgdl", LBXHDD = "HDL_mgdl", LBDHDD = "HDL_mgdl"). Ignored unit provided. unit Character NULL. unit string match column label attributes (case-insensitive). Common values: "mg/dL", "g/dL", "U/L", "umol/L". supplied, name must also provided. name Character NULL. output column name use unit supplied (e.g. "HDL_mgdl"). label_pattern Character NULL. additional regex matched column labels unit used. Use disambiguate file contains multiple columns unit (e.g. "HDL" select HDL column file also contains total cholesterol mg/dL). prefer_mgdl Logical. TRUE (default), drop SI-unit columns (mmol/L, g/L, etc.) conventional-unit counterpart (mg/dL, g/dL, U/L) exists data frame. Detection uses label attributes: unit token stripped label SI columns whose base description matches conventional-unit column removed. Applied renaming step. trim Logical. applies unit used. TRUE (default), returned data frame contains SEQN, cycle, column named name. Set FALSE retain columns (useful source file contains variables want keep). stack Logical. TRUE (default), row-bind renamed data frames single data frame using nhanes_stack(). Set FALSE return renamed list without stacking.

title: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
text: stack = TRUE (default), single stacked data frame. unit used trim = TRUE (default), SEQN, cycle, harmonized column returned — ready merging. stack = FALSE, named list data frames.

title: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
text: Unit-based (unit + name): scans column label attributes specified unit string (e.g. "mg/dL") renames matching column. need know CDC variable codes advance. Use label_pattern disambiguate file contains multiple columns unit (e.g. early cycles bundled total cholesterol HDL together). Explicit mapping (mapping): named character vector old -> new variable names. verbose unambiguous.

title: Harmonize variable names across NHANES cycles and stack into one data frame — nhanes_harmonize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cycles <- nhanes_cycles()[1:10, "cycle"]  # Unit-based: trim = TRUE (default) returns SEQN + cycle + HDL_mgdl only hdl_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("HDL", cycles) hdl <- nhanes_harmonize(hdl_list, unit = "mg/dL", name = "HDL_mgdl",                          label_pattern = "HDL")  # Merge two analytes cleanly tchol_list <- nhanes_download_analyte("total cholesterol", cycles) TC  <- nhanes_harmonize(tchol_list, unit = "mg/dL", name = "TC_mgdl",                          label_pattern = "total cholesterol") lipids <- merge(hdl, TC, by = c("SEQN", "cycle"))  # Explicit mapping (trim does not apply) hdl <- nhanes_harmonize(   hdl_list,   mapping = c(LBDHDL = "HDL_mgdl", LBXHDD = "HDL_mgdl",               LBDHDD = "HDL_mgdl") ) } # }

text: ... Two data frames nhanes_download(), containing SEQN column. multi-cycle datasets, also cycle column. Character vector join key(s). Default "SEQN". multi-cycle data, use c("SEQN", "cycle"). type Character. Join type: "inner" retains participants present files; "left" retains participants first file. Default "inner" (standard NHANES analytic approach). weight_var Character NULL. supplied, validates weight column exists merged result.

title: Merge NHANES component data frames by SEQN — nhanes_merge
text: appropriate weight depends components merged: Demographics → WTINT2YR (interview weight) exam/lab component → WTMEC2YR (MEC exam weight) Dietary 24-hr recall → WTDRD1 WTDR2D Multi-cycle pooled → divide 2-year weight number cycles, use 4-year combined weight available function warns enforce weight selection. Use nhanes_cycles() look available weight variable names per cycle.

title: Merge NHANES component data frames by SEQN — nhanes_merge
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ demo  <- nhanes_download("DEMO",  "2015-2016") bpx   <- nhanes_download("BPX",   "2015-2016") trigly <- nhanes_download("TRIGLY","2015-2016")  analytic <- nhanes_merge(demo, bpx, trigly)  # Multi-cycle demo_13 <- nhanes_download("DEMO", "2013-2014") demo_15 <- nhanes_download("DEMO", "2015-2016") bpx_13  <- nhanes_download("BPX",  "2013-2014") bpx_15  <- nhanes_download("BPX",  "2015-2016")  demo_pool <- rbind(demo_13, demo_15) bpx_pool  <- rbind(bpx_13,  bpx_15) analytic  <- nhanes_merge(demo_pool, bpx_pool, by = c("SEQN", "cycle")) } # }

title: Parse NHANES Linked Mortality Files into data frames — nhanes_mortality_parse
text: cycles Character vector cycle labels. Defaults available. refresh Logical. Re-parse even cached RDS exists? Default FALSE. download Logical. Auto-download missing .dat files? Default TRUE.

title: Parse NHANES Linked Mortality Files into data frames — nhanes_mortality_parse
text: named list data frames. data frame contains: SEQN Respondent sequence number (join key NHANES data). ELIGSTAT Eligibility: 1=eligible; 2=18; 3=insufficient data. MORTSTAT Vital status: 0=assumed alive; 1=assumed deceased. UCOD_LEADING Underlying cause death (11-category ICD-10 recode). DIABETES Diabetes mentioned death certificate (1=yes). HYPERTEN Hypertension mentioned death certificate (1=yes). PERMTH_INT Months follow-interview date. PERMTH_EXM Months follow-examination date.

title: nhanesR 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: nhanesR 0.1.0
text: nhanes_cycles() — list continuous NHANES cycles (1999–present) optionally NHANES III (1988–1994), cycle labels, begin/end years, file-name suffixes. nhanes_manifest() — query CDC data catalog available files within given cycle component. nhanes_download() — download one NHANES XPT files CDC, parse R data frames, cache locally. Falls back foreign::read.xport() haven::read_xpt() parse older files. nhanes_stack() — row-bind per-cycle data frames single data frame, filling columns absent cycles NA. nhanes_merge() — join NHANES components SEQN (optionally cycle), survey-design-aware defaults. nhanes_mortality_link() — download NCHS Public-Use Linked Mortality Files (LMF) relevant cycles left-join onto analytic dataset SEQN. Mortality follow-runs December 31, 2019. nhanes_survival_prep() — prepare survival analysis dataset mortality-linked data frame: removes ineligible participants (ELIGSTAT != 1), creates time event columns, optionally creates event_cause cause-specific mortality, warns asymmetric follow-across cycles. nhanes_followup_summary() — summarise follow-time event rates cycle. nhanes_ucod_labels() — return 11-category ICD-10 underlying cause-- death recode used public-use LMF. nhanes_search_variables() — search CDC NHANES variable catalog variables whose name description matches keyword. Results cached locally. summarize argument (default TRUE) collapses one-row-per-cycle output one row per unique variable name, comma-separated file names, cycles, n_cycles count. nhanes_variable_map() — wraps nhanes_search_variables() return single-row-per-cycle lookup table (cycle, variable_name, file_name) given analyte. Automatically drops comment-code variables, prefers non-SI variables exist cycle, accepts keep_vars argument disambiguate serum urine forms analyte (e.g. serum creatinine vs. urinary creatinine). nhanes_cache_dir() — view change local cache directory.

title: nhanesR 0.1.0
text: Fixed CDC data file URLs: CDC reorganized NHANES file paths /Nchs/Nhanes/{cycle}/ /Nchs/Data/Nhanes/Public/{begin_year}/DataFiles/. download functions updated accordingly. Added Content-Type check HTTP download helper: CDC returns HTTP 200 HTML error page file moved; nhanesR now detects aborts informative message rather saving corrupt file. Added foreign::read.xport() fallback XPT files haven::read_xpt() parse (affects files NHANES cycles prior 2003). MD5 hash sidecar files (.md5) written alongside every cached RDS detect corruption trigger re-download needed.
